DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-21-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. WALT CUBBERLY, Esq. of Williams Hart Boundas Easterby, LLP (Tel: 713-230-2200) for plaintiff;
Attys. GARY A. WOLENSKY, Esq. and PAUL A. ALARCON, Esq. of Buchalter (Tel: 949-760-1121) for defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. and Rasier, LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. AND RASIER, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. and Rasier, LLC's demurrers to causes of action 4-7 (assault, sexual battery, false imprisonment, intentional infliction of emotional distress) are overruled. Defendants' demurrers ignore the complaint's allegations that "Uber is a common carrier that must carry passengers safely. As a common carrier, Uber is liable for its employees and agents' intentional and negligent torts, whether such acts were committed within the scope of employment." (Cmpl., par. 116.) These allegations may or may not prove factually founded, but that is not for resolution at the pleading state. This demurrers are overruled.

Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-21-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. WALT CUBBERLY, Esq. of Williams Hart Boundas Easterby, LLP (Tel: 713-230-2200) for plaintiff;
Attys. GARY A. WOLENSKY, Esq. and PAUL A. ALARCON, Esq. of Buchalter (Tel: 949-760-1121) for defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. and Rasier, LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. AND RASIER, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. and Rasier, LLC's motion to strike is denied. Plaintiff's colorful allegations are within the realm of acceptable pleading, particularly for background. Because the basis for vicarious liability is adequately pled on the basis of the common carrier allegations, that part of the motion is denied. The court denies the motion as to the other punitive damages allegations. Plaintiff pleads sufficient ultimate facts, including allegations of ratification, to support that relief. (See, e.g., Cmpl., pars. 1, 24-26, 29, 35, 43-44.)

Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
